summary:
The main constituents of porcelain include silica and feldspar; thus, calcium carbonate is absent from this material as per standard ceramic compositions.
The absence of amorphous substances like those found occasionally added for specific purposes further confirms that calcium carbonate is notably missing in true porcelain formulation. Agent1 reasoned that since porcelain contains silica and feldspar while lacking significant amounts of calcium carbonate used more commonly in limestones or similar products, option C stands out as correct by being excluded based on typical porcelain ingredients. This summary captures the essence without extraneous information about optional additives. \boxed{\text{Calcium Carbonate}} was identified correctly through analysis focusing solely on fundamental characteristics defining porcelain versus alternative minerals.
